element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each character has unique interaction capabilities; swapping needed for puzzle progression
Pages placed in hard-to-reach areas; hidden pages require object interaction or character actions
Required to reveal hidden script pages and reach elevated platforms
Essential for accessing higher platforms; three-character stack stronger than two-character

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy encounters (small robots, Ralph the Security Guard)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Ralph captures characters; defeat triggers rescue stages
Required to progress; defeated bosses reappear in final Editing Room boss rush
Mandatory mini-stages to recover captured/defeated characters
Activates power-ups: temporary invincibility, character recovery, coin manipulation
Contextual theming; affects visual presentation and enemy variety

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Final stage (unlocked after all other stages complete)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cumulative challenge featuring all previous bosses followed by Pinky and Brain final battle

typical_relief_triggers: Successful character stacking to reach new areas
Rescuing captured characters via rescue stages
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple character defeats requiring rescue sequences
Final boss rush in Editing Room
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-driven with escalating challenge; script collection creates optional tension (multiple endings based on page count); mandatory boss encounters at stage climax punctuate progression; final rush consolidates all bosses into climactic sequence

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Script pages placed in hard-to-reach areas and hidden locations; collected count determines ending quality (best ending requires collecting all 24 pages)
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden script pages revealed through object manipulation or specific character interactions; non-linear distribution across stages rewards exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to character swapping, basic platforming, and script collection mechanics; safe introduction context with moderate complexity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy variety (small robots, Ralph encounters); stacking mechanics become essential; hard-to-reach script pages require complex problem-solving
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters escalate in difficulty; Editing Room boss rush forces mastery of all previous boss patterns sequentially
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (transitions from exploration to combat requirement)
Editing Room boss rush (cumulative challenge with all bosses plus final Pinky/Brain battle)
Ralph encounters (character capture mechanic changes rescue-stage availability)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage parodies different film genres with unique visual themes, enemies, and mechanics while maintaining core platforming and script collection objectives
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Science Fiction/Space Opera stages; Action Movie stage (Once There Was A Man Named Oscar)

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages emphasize exploration and hidden script page discovery, concluding with mandatory boss encounter; victory unlocks subsequent stage or final area
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All primary stages before Editing Room

notable_level_patterns.description: When character(s) defeated or captured, game pauses main progression and inserts mandatory rescue mini-stage with simplified objectives; recovery restores main stage continuation
Final stage consolidates all previously defeated bosses into sequential encounters, followed by ultimate Pinky and Brain confrontation; tests cumulative mastery
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Editing Room (unlocked after all other stages complete)

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Animaniacs SNES development info, test levels, mechanics)
wikipedia - Animaniacs (video game) (gameplay mechanics, SNES-specific features, character abilities, script collection system, ending variations)
